# sql_corpus.txt — pg_query equivalence harness corpus (SC-008).
# 100+ SQL statements, one per non-blank non-# line.
# Used by tests/pg_query_equivalence.rs to assert the AST-backed
# has_returning agrees with pg_query's direct AST answer on each line.
# Phase 3 will extend this corpus with extract_tables and
# extract_filter_columns oracles.
SELECT 1
SELECT * FROM users
SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = 42
SELECT id, name FROM users ORDER BY id LIMIT 10 OFFSET 20
SELECT u.id, u.name, p.title FROM users u INNER JOIN posts p ON p.user_id = u.id
SELECT count(*) FROM users GROUP BY created_at::date HAVING count(*) > 10
SELECT * FROM users u LEFT JOIN (SELECT user_id, max(created_at) AS last FROM logs GROUP BY user_id) l ON l.user_id = u.id
SELECT u.id, (SELECT count(*) FROM posts p WHERE p.user_id = u.id) AS post_count FROM users u
SELECT id, sum(total) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY ts) AS running FROM payments
SELECT DISTINCT ON (user_id) user_id, id FROM events ORDER BY user_id, ts DESC
SELECT u.id, p.* FROM users u, LATERAL (SELECT * FROM posts WHERE user_id = u.id ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 3) p
SELECT data->'email' FROM users WHERE data @> '{"active":true}'
SELECT * FROM t WHERE x IN (SELECT y FROM s WHERE s.z > 100)
SELECT x, sum(y) FROM t GROUP BY GROUPING SETS ((x), (x, y), ())
SELECT datname FROM pg_catalog.pg_database WHERE datname NOT LIKE 'template%'
SELECT * FROM t WHERE id BETWEEN 10 AND 20 AND name LIKE 'foo%'
SELECT * FROM t TABLESAMPLE BERNOULLI (10)
SELECT current_setting('server_version')
SELECT generate_series(1, 10) AS s
SELECT array_agg(id ORDER BY created_at) FROM events
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES (1)
INSERT INTO t (a, b) VALUES (1, 2), (3, 4), (5, 6)
INSERT INTO t (a) SELECT x FROM s WHERE y > 100
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES (1) ON CONFLICT (a) DO NOTHING
INSERT INTO t (a, b) VALUES (1, 2) ON CONFLICT (a) DO UPDATE SET b = EXCLUDED.b
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES (1) RETURNING id
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES (1), (2) RETURNING id, a
INSERT INTO t DEFAULT VALUES
INSERT INTO t DEFAULT VALUES RETURNING id
INSERT INTO public.t (a) VALUES (1)
INSERT INTO analytics.events (ts, kind) VALUES (now(), 'click')
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES (1) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING id
INSERT INTO t (a) SELECT x FROM s RETURNING t.*
INSERT INTO t (a) SELECT x FROM s WHERE y IN (SELECT z FROM u) RETURNING id
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES (DEFAULT)
INSERT INTO t (a, b) VALUES (1, DEFAULT), (DEFAULT, 2)
INSERT INTO t SELECT * FROM s
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES ('''with quote''')
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES (E'raw\nwith newline')
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES ($$dollar$$)
INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES ($tag$tagged$tag$)
UPDATE t SET a = 1
UPDATE t SET a = 1 WHERE id = 10
UPDATE t SET a = 1, b = 2 WHERE id = 10 RETURNING id
UPDATE t SET a = (SELECT max(x) FROM s WHERE s.y = t.id) WHERE active
UPDATE t SET a = u.a FROM u WHERE t.id = u.id
UPDATE t SET a = 1 WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM s)
UPDATE public.t SET a = 1 WHERE id = 10
UPDATE t SET a = a + 1
UPDATE t SET data = data || '{"x":1}' WHERE id = 10
UPDATE t SET a = CASE WHEN b > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
UPDATE t SET a = 1 RETURNING *
UPDATE t SET a = 1 WHERE updated_at < now() - interval '1 day'
UPDATE orders SET status = 'cancelled' WHERE customer_id = 42 RETURNING id, total
UPDATE t SET tsv = to_tsvector(content) WHERE id = 1
UPDATE t SET a = 1 FROM s WHERE t.id = s.id AND s.y > 100 RETURNING t.id
DELETE FROM t
DELETE FROM t WHERE id = 10
DELETE FROM t WHERE id IN (1, 2, 3)
DELETE FROM t USING s WHERE t.id = s.id
DELETE FROM t WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM s)
DELETE FROM t RETURNING id
DELETE FROM t WHERE created_at < now() - interval '30 days' RETURNING *
DELETE FROM public.t WHERE id = 10
DELETE FROM orders WHERE status = 'cancelled' RETURNING id
DELETE FROM t WHERE a IS NULL
DELETE FROM t WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM s WHERE s.t_id = t.id)
DELETE FROM t USING s, u WHERE t.id = s.t_id AND s.u_id = u.id
DELETE FROM t WHERE ctid = (SELECT ctid FROM t LIMIT 1)
DELETE FROM t WHERE updated_at < now() - interval '1 day' RETURNING id, updated_at
DELETE FROM analytics.events WHERE ts < now() - interval '90 days'
WITH x AS (SELECT 1 AS id) SELECT * FROM x
WITH RECURSIVE r AS (SELECT 1 AS n UNION ALL SELECT n + 1 FROM r WHERE n < 10) SELECT * FROM r
WITH x AS MATERIALIZED (SELECT * FROM s) SELECT * FROM x
WITH x AS (INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES (1) RETURNING id) SELECT * FROM x
WITH x AS (UPDATE t SET a = 1 RETURNING id) SELECT * FROM x
WITH x AS (DELETE FROM t WHERE id = 1 RETURNING id) SELECT * FROM x
WITH x AS (INSERT INTO t (a) VALUES (1)) SELECT 1
WITH x AS (SELECT * FROM t), y AS (SELECT * FROM s) SELECT x.*, y.* FROM x JOIN y ON true
WITH x AS (SELECT 1) INSERT INTO t (a) SELECT * FROM x
WITH x AS (SELECT 1) DELETE FROM t WHERE id IN (SELECT * FROM x)
CREATE TABLE t (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name text NOT NULL)
ALTER TABLE t ADD COLUMN created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()
CREATE INDEX idx_t_name ON t (name)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_t_email ON t (email) WHERE email IS NOT NULL
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS old_t
TRUNCATE t
CREATE TABLE child () INHERITS (parent)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv AS SELECT * FROM t
ALTER TABLE t ALTER COLUMN name SET NOT NULL
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v AS SELECT id, name FROM t
MERGE INTO t USING s ON t.id = s.id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET a = s.a WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT VALUES (s.id, s.a)
MERGE INTO t USING s ON t.id = s.id WHEN MATCHED THEN DELETE
MERGE INTO t USING (SELECT * FROM u) AS s ON t.id = s.id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET a = s.a WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN DO NOTHING
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM t WHERE id = 10
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT count(*) FROM t
VACUUM t
VACUUM (FULL, ANALYZE) t
SELECT setval('t_id_seq', 100)
SELECT query FROM pg_stat_statements WHERE total_exec_time > 1000 LIMIT 10
SELECT * FROM pg_locks WHERE NOT granted
SELECT pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size('t'))
